author: PC Gamer
text:
Steam is getting a jockey simulator next month that reimagines umamusume as normal horses
It's an unconventional idea, but I'm willing to withhold judgement.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fresh off of dumping on Xbox's flailing, former Sony exec says PlayStation's pullback from PC releases doesn't make any sense either
Shawn Layden is a man with opinions, and now that he's out of the game he doesn't mind sharing them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Ahead of next week's expected Xbox bloodbath, IO Interactive loses funding for its online fantasy RPG
The studio says it remains "100% committed" to Project Fantasy, but its future is now very uncertain.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Open source game engine Godot will no longer accept AI-authored code contributions: 'We can't trust heavy users of AI to understand their code enough to fix it'
At risk of drowning in AI slop code, Godot is firming up its contribution requirements.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Before his death in 2025, Half-Life 2 art director Viktor Antonov worked on one last game: A Wild West FPS soulslike that's 'coming soon' to Steam
Antonov's bold vision helped define games including Half-Life 2, Dishonored, Weird West, and soon, Guns of Eschaton.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve says it is 'definitely' investigating an Arm-based gaming future on top of its work on FEX
Flexing ahead.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I can barely afford AMD and Nvidia's current 4 nm chips, so I'm not sure what to make of IBM's new sub-1 nm technology
Moore's Law might not be dead, but it is kinda zombified.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The latest SteamOS beta update includes a performance boost for GPUs with limited VRAM, though devices with iGPUs probably won't see any gains
But if the device is a certain Machine, then this is definitely good news.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Russian politician says GTA 6 carries 'the stench of Americanism,' but it's not so bad because the games are all about Americans killing each other
"Any violence in GTA is violence against a potential enemy."

author: PC Gamer
text:
'We finally have some good news for you': Framework's new 1 TB Gen 5 SSDs are actually cheaper than its old 500 GB drives
Dare we hope this is a sign of things to come...?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve doesn't currently have 'any specific plans' for more Steam Machine faceplates, so you'll have to make them yourself
Seems like a problem a lot of glitter can fix.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Be Quiet Dark Rock 6 and Dark Rock Pro 6 review
Better, but at what cost?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Turtle Beach Command Series KB7 TKL review
Turtle Beach puts a screen on another screenless PC mainstay.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Kingdom Come: Deliverance 2 team reminds you it's got another Kingdom Come and a Middle-earth game on the way as it tops 6,000,000 sales
Radzig Kobyla's sword vs Anduril. It's the fight of the 15th century.

author: PC Gamer
text:
AMD's next-gen Zen 6 CPU cores will come in a low-power compact flavour, which should help laptop batteries last even longer
Intel's Panther Lake has similar LPE cores, and they work a treat.

author: PC Gamer
text:
ESA bafflingly declares private Minecraft servers 'illegal' in Stop Killing Games hearing: 'We consider it piracy, we have lawsuits'
Catch me on the corner selling server.jar out of a long trenchcoat.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'99% of the work was already done in a sense': Valve says it has less to fix with the Steam Machine because they already fixed it on the Steam Deck
Dust yourself off and try again.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Valve says the memory and storage crisis is so bad, it's not just a problem of pricing, they had to 'negotiate really hard' just to secure supply for the Steam Machine
One day, we'll laugh about all this. Maybe...

author: PC Gamer
text:
New production runs of Nvidia's much-loved RTX 3060 finally hit the shelves in Europe, but the price just ain't right
Ignore how much VRAM it has; it's simply not worth 333 euros.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dragon Age: Origins is 'Unsupported' on Steam Deck, but it runs incredibly and it's currently only $3
After some easy setting up, BioWare's fantasy classic sings on Deck.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Persona could be getting the Netflix live action treatment, which means, oh no, Americans will be making it
Talent behind Stranger Things, Sonic The Hedgehog and Star Trek: Picard are reportedly involved.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hearing about the future of PlayStation from its top dogs made me so depressed I had to go lie down
My life for shareholder value.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dawn of War 2’s The Last Stand mode is still the best co-op MOBA that never was
A co-op Warhammer 40K RTS mode so ahead of its time, it's still easy to find a match 17 years later.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After leading the studio through 2 rounds of layoffs and 1 game release, the CEO of Supermassive is stepping down
He's also stepping down from his role at Supermassive parent Nordisk, so he can spend the summer with his family.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Xbox marketing takes embarrassing turn with new Call of Duty ads loudly disclaiming 'NOT ON XBOX GAME PASS THIS YEAR'
My 'NOT ON XBOX GAME PASS THIS YEAR' ad has people wondering a lot of things about my business strategy already clarified by my ad.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Hideki Kamiya thinks there should be a cozy Resident Evil game, where 'retired Leon goes fishing in the countryside'
It's cool if someone else makes it, as long as he gets to play it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
World's smartest Meccha Chameleon player bamboozles opponents by not disguising themselves at all: 'Hiding is for amateurs'
There is an imposter among us.

author: PC Gamer
text:
All +1 Speed Keyboard Escape codes for June 2026: Extra speed
All active +1 Speed Keyboard Escape codes for free speed boosts and multipliers.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New Vegas director Josh Sawyer says games can and should pull on artistic inspirations from 'anywhere and everywhere': 'Greek plays, they knocked it out of the park thousands of years ago—why not look at them?'
If you want the player to feel something, look to media that already cracked the code.

author: PC Gamer
text:
5 years after we called this Kickstarted MMO 'a giant fiasco' and just 3 months after it finally launched, it's shutting down
Even the shutdown process is turning into a mess.
